title: Sitharam–Willoughby Conjecture on excluded minors for $f_\infty(G) \leq 2$
statement_text: $W_4$ is an excluded minor for $f_\infty(G) \leq 2$, and $W_4$ is the only excluded minor for $f_\infty(G) \leq 2$.
context_text: Using techniques from rigidity matroids, Sitharam and Willoughby determined $f_\infty(G)$ for all graphs $G$ with at most 5 vertices, except for $W_4$. They then posed two conjectures: first that $W_4$ is an excluded minor for $f_\infty(G) \leq 2$, and second that $W_4$ is the only such excluded minor. The present paper takes these conjectures as its central subject.
paper_contribution: Verifies the first conjecture ($W_4$ is indeed an excluded minor for $f_\infty(G) \leq 2$) but disproves the second by showing that $K_4 +_e K_4$ is also an excluded minor; the complete set of excluded minors for $f_\infty(G) \leq 2$ is exactly $\{W_4,\, K_4 +_e K_4\}$ (Theorem 3).
notes: Stated in running prose without a labelled theorem environment; attributed to Sitharam and Willoughby [17] but the bibliography is absent from the extracted text so the year cannot be determined. PDF source — math may be garbled in places.
paper_title: The excluded minors for isometric realizability in the plane
